ranked_movies.title: Weapons
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed and debated across multiple subreddits, particularly in r/TrueFilm, for its narrative structure, thematic depth around addiction, and polarizing reception. High visibility and engagement make it the most prominent film.

ranked_movies.title: Sorry to Bother You
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended for its insane, jaw-dropping twists and unique narrative. Described as a must-watch blind experience, generating significant positive engagement.

ranked_movies.title: Melancholia
ranked_movies.reason: A deeply analyzed film in r/TrueFilm for its portrayal of depression and existential themes, sparking extensive discussion about its characters and structure.

ranked_movies.title: Cabin in the Woods
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently suggested as a top pick for insane, blind-viewing experiences due to its unexpected plot twists and meta-commentary on horror tropes.

ranked_movies.title: Mandy
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as peak Nicolas Cage and a visually stunning, insane film that benefits from going in blind, often cited in the same breath as other cult classics.

ranked_movies.title: One Cut of the Dead
ranked_movies.reason: Strongly recommended with an emphasis on trusting the recommendation and going in blind, noted for its unique structure and surprising payoff.

ranked_movies.title: From Dusk Till Dawn
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently recommended for blind viewing due to its genre-bending twist, with warnings to avoid even the cover art to preserve the surprise.

ranked_movies.title: Predestination
ranked_movies.reason: Often paired with 'Coherence' as a mind-bending thriller that leaves viewers stunned, frequently mentioned in threads seeking shocking twists.

ranked_movies.title: Existenz
ranked_movies.reason: Top-voted recommendation in the 'insane movies' thread, recognized for its weird and shocking themes that demand a blind viewing.

ranked_movies.title: The Fall
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times for its visual splendor and stunning cinematography, ideal for big-screen viewing, generating significant agreement and support.

ranked_movies.title: Silkwood
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of its own highly-upvoted post, praised for its powerful true story and impactful filmmaking that 'stays with you forever'.

ranked_movies.title: Blade Runner 2049
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited alongside the original as a top-tier visually stunning film, with specific praise for its cinematography and direction.

ranked_movies.title: Speak No Evil
ranked_movies.reason: Top recommendation for a thriller that delivers a shocking, jaw-dropping experience, specifically noted for its impactful ending.

ranked_movies.title: Shame
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted in discussions about sexual frustration and loveless sex, praised for Michael Fassbender's performance and its raw portrayal of addiction.

ranked_movies.title: Poor Things
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed for its copious yet non-titillating sexual content, noted for being dark and remarkable in its approach to sexuality.

ranked_movies.title: Barry Lyndon
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its visual mastery, with specific advice to watch it on an OLED screen, indicating high regard for its cinematography.

ranked_movies.title: The Game
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a classic thriller that delivers a mind-blowing experience and a stunning finale, often listed among the best in the genre.

ranked_movies.title: Ex Machina
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a thriller that 'got me good,' recognized for its intelligent plot and shocking twists that leave a lasting impression.

ranked_movies.title: Pan's Labyrinth
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its visual beauty and stunning fantasy elements, frequently mentioned in threads seeking cinematographic excellence.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the context of both thrillers and sexual themes, noted for its consequential and shocking use of sex in the plot.
